name: Use Redis as a Session Store
description: Store and manage user sessions in Redis for fast, centralized session handling across multiple application instances.

steps.title: Create a session key with user data
steps.description: Store session data as a hash with a unique session ID and set a TTL for automatic expiration.
steps.command: redis-cli HSET session:abc123 userId 1001 username alice role admin createdAt '2026-03-11T10:00:00Z'

steps.title: Set session expiration
steps.description: Automatically expire the session after 30 minutes of inactivity.
steps.tip: Call EXPIRE again on each request to implement sliding session windows.

steps.title: Retrieve session data
steps.description: Fetch all fields from a session hash to validate the user.

steps.title: Destroy a session on logout
steps.description: Remove the session key entirely when the user logs out.

steps.title: Count active sessions
steps.description: Use SCAN to count all active session keys without blocking.
steps.warning: Avoid KEYS in production as it blocks the server. Always use SCAN for pattern matching on live instances.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Redis Session Store Management
set -euo pipefail

SESSION_ID="sess_$(openssl rand -hex 16)"
echo "=== Creating session: $SESSION_ID ==="
redis-cli HSET "session:$SESSION_ID" userId 1001 username alice role admin createdAt "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
redis-cli EXPIRE "session:$SESSION_ID" 1800

echo ""
echo "=== Session data ==="
redis-cli HGETALL "session:$SESSION_ID"

echo ""
echo "=== TTL remaining ==="
redis-cli TTL "session:$SESSION_ID"

echo ""
echo "=== Active sessions ==="
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'session:*' | wc -l

echo ""
echo "=== Destroying session ==="
redis-cli DEL "session:$SESSION_ID"
echo "Session destroyed."

faq.question: Hash vs string for session data?
faq.answer: Hashes let you read or update individual fields (e.g., HGET session:id username) without deserializing the entire session. Strings with JSON are simpler but require full read/write cycles.

faq.question: How do I implement sliding session expiration?
faq.answer: Call EXPIRE on the session key with each authenticated request. This resets the TTL so sessions only expire after a period of inactivity, not a fixed time from creation.
